recipes.name: Lemon Garlic Salmon
recipes.description: Juicy air fryer salmon fillets with crispy skin, bright lemon, and caramelized garlic. Fast, clean, high-protein.

steps.instruction: Pat salmon dry with paper towel. Season with salt, pepper, and paprika.
steps.tip: Dry skin = crispy skin. Don't skip this step.

steps.instruction: Mix minced garlic with olive oil and lemon zest. Brush over salmon.
Preheat air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes.
Place salmon skin-side down in air fryer basket. Cook 10-12 min.
steps.tip: No need to flip — the air circulates all around.

steps.instruction: Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the top before serving.

airFryerSettings.notes: No flipping needed. Check at 10 min for thinner fillets.

storageNotes.portionNotes: Pack second portion immediately after cooking to preserve moisture.

recipes.name: Teriyaki Tuna Rice Bowl
recipes.nameEs: Bowl de Atún Teriyaki con Arroz
recipes.description: Seared tuna steak glazed in homemade teriyaki, served over steamed rice with sesame cucumber salad.

steps.instruction: Cook rice according to package instructions. Keep warm.
Mix soy sauce, honey, and a splash of water in a small bowl to make teriyaki glaze.
Pat tuna steak dry. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear tuna 2 min per side for medium.
steps.tip: High heat = sear, not steam. The pan must be smoking.

steps.instruction: Pour teriyaki glaze into the pan and let reduce 1 min, coating the tuna.
Slice cucumber thin. Toss with a few drops of sesame oil and sesame seeds.
Serve tuna sliced over rice. Add cucumber salad and garnish with spring onion.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Microwave rice + cold tuna sliced over top (tuna is better cold)
storageNotes.portionNotes: Store tuna and rice separately to avoid mushiness.

recipes.name: Mediterranean Sea Bass
recipes.description: Whole sea bass roasted with olives, cherry tomatoes, capers, and herbs. Simple, elegant, packed with flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Toss cherry tomatoes, olives, capers, and garlic with olive oil in a baking dish.
Season sea bass with salt, pepper, and thyme. Nestle into the vegetables.
Lay lemon slices over the fish. Drizzle with a little more olive oil.
Roast 20-25 minutes until fish flakes easily.
Let rest 2 minutes before serving.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Oven at 160°C for 8 min covered with foil
storageNotes.portionNotes: The tomato sauce keeps the second portion very moist.

proteinBuffer.name: Hard-boiled eggs
proteinBuffer.description: 2 hard-boiled eggs with a pinch of salt

recipes.name: Lemon Tuna Pasta alla Mediterranea
recipes.nameEs: Pasta con Atún al Limón Mediterránea
recipes.description: A bright, satisfying Italian-style pasta with albacore tuna, capers, cherry tomatoes, and lemon zest — clean, bold flavors with serious protein punch.

ingredients.name: Cherry tomatoes, halved
ingredients.nameEs: Tomates cherry, partidos por la mitad

steps.instruction: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve 60ml (¼ cup) of pasta cooking water before draining.
steps.tip: Salting the water well is your only chance to season the pasta itself — it should taste like the sea.

steps.instruction: While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced garlic and chili flakes, cooking gently until fragrant and just golden. Do not let the garlic brown.
steps.tip: Low and slow on the garlic — bitter garlic ruins the whole dish.

steps.instruction: Add the cherry tomatoes to the skillet. Cook, pressing them lightly with a spoon, until they begin to burst and release their juices.
Add the drained tuna and capers to the skillet. Break the tuna apart gently with a fork and stir to combine with the tomato mixture. Cook for 1–2 minutes to warm through.
steps.tip: Keep some tuna chunks intact for texture — avoid over-stirring.

steps.instruction: Add the drained pasta to the skillet. Toss everything together, adding a splash of the reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce and help it cling to the pasta. Add lemon zest and juice, and toss again.
steps.tip: The starchy pasta water is the secret to a silky, cohesive sauce without needing cream or butter.

steps.instruction: Remove from heat. Stir in the chopped parsley, adjust seasoning with salt and pepper, and serve immediately.

storageNotes.reheatingMethod: Reheat in a skillet over medium-low heat with a small splash of water to loosen the pasta and prevent sticking. Avoid microwave if possible as it dries the pasta out.
storageNotes.portionNotes: Double all quantities when cooking. Store the second portion in an airtight container once fully cooled. This also works great eaten cold as a pasta salad for lunch.

proteinBuffer.name: Hard-Boiled Eggs + Rice Cakes
proteinBuffer.description: Two hard-boiled eggs with two plain rice cakes — a light, no-fuss morning snack that keeps you satiated and complements the Mediterranean flavors of the day without adding dairy or meat.
